name: Astrophysics & Cosmology
description: Stars, galaxies, cosmology, dark matter, and dark energy. Astrophysics applies physics to the cosmos — from stellar structure and evolution to the large-scale structure of the universe. Cosmology seeks to understand the origin, evolution, and ultimate fate of the universe as a whole.
axioms: Stellar structure: hydrostatic equilibrium (dP/dr = -Gm(r)ρ/r²) balanced by energy transport (radiation, convection, conduction)
Hertzsprung-Russell diagram: stars are classified by luminosity and surface temperature — main sequence, giants, white dwarfs
Hubble's law: v = H₀d — galaxies recede from us at a velocity proportional to their distance (cosmological expansion)
Friedmann equations: govern the expansion of the universe from GR under the assumptions of homogeneity and isotropy
Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB): blackbody radiation at T = 2.725 K — relic radiation from ~380,000 years after the Big Bang
Dark matter: ~27% of the universe's mass-energy — inferred from gravitational effects (galaxy rotation curves, gravitational lensing)
Dark energy: ~68% of the universe — drives the accelerating expansion (cosmological constant or dynamical field)
reasoning_patterns: Spectroscopic analysis: identify elements, temperature, density, and radial velocity from absorption/emission line spectra
Light curve analysis: extract binary parameters, stellar pulsation periods, exoplanet transits, and supernova timing
Cosmological distance ladder: parallax (Gaia) → Cepheid variables → Type Ia supernovae → baryon acoustic oscillations
Nucleosynthesis tracking: trace element formation from Big Bang (H, He, Li) through stellar evolution (C, N, O, Fe) to supernovae (r-process, s-process)
N-body/hydrodynamic simulation: model gravitational dynamics of dark matter halos, galaxy formation, and cluster mergers
CMB power spectrum analysis: extract cosmological parameters (Ω_m, Ω_Λ, H₀, n_s, τ) from angular power spectrum peaks
Gravitational wave astronomy: extract source parameters (masses, spins, distance) from waveform templates
boundary_conditions: Dark matter particle identity is unknown — only gravitational effects are observed
Dark energy mechanism is unknown — could be cosmological constant, quintessence, or modified gravity
Black hole information paradox remains unresolved (Hawking radiation vs. unitary evolution)
First stars (Population III, metal-free) have not yet been directly observed
Inflationary cosmology is a paradigm, not a unique theory — many models fit current data
The Hubble tension (~5σ discrepancy in H₀ measurements) is unresolved
key_references: Carroll & Ostlie. An Introduction to Modern Astrophysics
Peebles, P.J.E. Principles of Physical Cosmology
canonical_truths: The Sun is a G2V main-sequence star, ~4.6 billion years old — one of ~100 billion stars in the Milky Way
The observable universe is ~93 billion light-years in diameter (comoving distance)
Age of the universe: ~13.8 billion years (Planck 2018 — ΛCDM best fit)
Neutron star density: ~10¹⁷ kg/m³ (nuclear density — a teaspoon weighs billions of tons)
Gravitational waves from binary mergers have been detected by LIGO/Virgo since 2015 — opening a new observational window
The cosmic microwave background is the most perfect blackbody spectrum ever measured
The universe contains ~2 trillion galaxies (Hubble Ultra Deep Field + deep surveys)
